description: Lock Recorded Music Industry to commercial phonograph/cylinder recording and sale rather than newspaper publicity or generic mass media.

checks.reason: The Library of Congress sources tie the industry's first commercial products to phonograph machines and cylinder records.
Newspapers may advertise records, but the inspected sources do not make newspaper circulation a prerequisite for the recorded-music industry.
Copyright and printing privileges are retained only as legal-rights ancestry for later recorded-music copyright management.
The node remains anchored to the LOC date for commercial musical-entertainment cylinder production.
